models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.description: Best for complex agents, multi-step planning, and difficult engineering. Top choice for OpenClaw autonomous workflows.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4
models.description: Balanced frontier model. Best everyday choice for Claude Code. Strong coding, fast enough, reasonable cost.

models.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
models.description: Budget Anthropic option. Fast and cheap for triage, quick edits, and support tasks.

models.name: GPT-4.1
models.description: OpenAI flagship. Strong for Codex CLI workflows, large repos, and tool-heavy agents.

models.name: o3
models.description: Reasoning-first model. Best for analysis, audits, and deliberate planning in agent loops.

models.name: o3-mini
models.description: Budget reasoning model. Good for structured problem solving without premium pricing.

models.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
models.description: Large-context multimodal. Strong for repo analysis, docs synthesis, and research-heavy workflows.

models.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
models.description: Fast budget Gemini. Good for quick reviews, routing, and lightweight assistance.

models.name: DeepSeek V3
models.description: Best value coding model. Strong performance at a fraction of frontier pricing. Works well with local tools.

models.name: DeepSeek R1
models.description: Budget reasoning model. Excellent for agent evaluation loops and structured analysis.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.description: Terminal-first coding agent. Best for implementation, refactors, and repo-aware edits. Pairs best with Claude models.

harnesses.name: OpenClaw
harnesses.description: Composable agent runtime. Best for autonomous workflows, multi-step orchestration, and reusable skills.

harnesses.name: Codex CLI
harnesses.description: OpenAI terminal agent. Strong for editing, repo inspection, and explanation loops.

harnesses.name: Gemini CLI
harnesses.description: Google CLI tool. Good for long-context review and multimodal tasks.

harnesses.name: Aider
harnesses.description: Git-aware terminal assistant. Best budget option. Works great with DeepSeek and local models.

usecases.name: Claude Code
usecases.description: Best combos for Claude Code terminal workflows. Implementation, refactors, and repo editing.

usecases.name: OpenClaw
usecases.description: Best combos for OpenClaw autonomous agents. Multi-step orchestration and skills.

usecases.name: Budget
usecases.description: Best value stacks. Good quality without premium pricing.

usecases.name: Local-First
usecases.description: Best for self-hosted or local workflows. Privacy and control.

combos.name: Claude Opus 4 + Claude Code
combos.description: Premium implementation stack. Best when you need deep reasoning for complex refactors and multi-file changes.
combos.notes: Top choice when edit quality matters more than cost. Use for difficult engineering tasks.
cons: Slower than Sonnet for simple tasks

combos.name: Claude Sonnet 4 + Claude Code
combos.description: Balanced everyday stack. Best all-around choice for Claude Code users.
combos.notes: Sweet spot of quality, speed, and cost. Default choice for most Claude Code workflows.
cons: Less depth than Opus on hardest tasks

combos.name: Claude Opus 4 + OpenClaw
combos.description: Best autonomous agent stack. Deep planning plus composable skills and orchestration.
combos.notes: Top choice for autonomous agents that need deliberate planning and tool orchestration.

combos.name: Claude Sonnet 4 + OpenClaw
combos.description: Balanced agent stack. Good for most autonomous workflows without premium cost.
combos.notes: Solid default for OpenClaw agents. Good balance of reasoning and speed.
cons: Less depth than Opus for complex planning

combos.name: o3 + OpenClaw
combos.description: Reasoning-focused agent stack. Best for analysis, audits, and evaluation loops.
combos.notes: Excellent for agents that need to think carefully before acting.

combos.name: DeepSeek R1 + OpenClaw
combos.description: Budget reasoning agent. Excellent value for autonomous workflows.
combos.notes: Best value for OpenClaw agents. Good reasoning at a fraction of frontier cost.

combos.name: DeepSeek V3 + Aider
combos.description: Best budget local stack. Strong coding at minimal cost with git-aware workflows.
combos.notes: Top budget choice. Works great for implementation when cost matters.

combos.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku + Claude Code
combos.description: Budget Claude Code stack. Fast and cheap for quick edits and triage.
combos.notes: Good for low-stakes work where speed and cost matter more than depth.

combos.name: GPT-4.1 + Codex CLI
combos.description: OpenAI flagship stack. Strong for large repos and tool-heavy workflows.
combos.notes: Best OpenAI-native stack. Good alternative if you prefer GPT over Claude.

combos.name: o3-mini + Codex CLI
combos.description: Budget OpenAI reasoning stack. Good for structured problem solving.
combos.notes: Good value for reasoning-heavy workflows without premium cost.

combos.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro + Gemini CLI
combos.description: Google flagship stack. Best for long-context analysis and multimodal workflows.
combos.notes: Strong for research-heavy agent workflows with large context needs.
cons: CLI less mature than Claude Code

combos.name: DeepSeek V3 + OpenClaw
combos.description: Budget coding agent. Strong value for implementation-focused workflows.
combos.notes: Good budget option for OpenClaw when coding quality matters more than reasoning depth.

combos.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku + OpenClaw
combos.description: Budget Claude agent. Fast and cheap for lightweight autonomous tasks.
combos.notes: Good for simple agent loops where cost and speed matter more than depth.

combos.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash + Gemini CLI
combos.description: Budget Google stack. Fast and cheap for reviews and lightweight tasks.
combos.notes: Good value for quick reviews and routing tasks.

combos.name: DeepSeek R1 + Aider
combos.description: Budget reasoning + git-aware editing. Good for analysis-heavy coding.
combos.notes: Good for coding tasks that need more reasoning than V3 provides.
